programme.title: D-Day
programme.desc: The planning for the Allied invasion on June 6th 1944 took two years and cost thousands of lives, and involved a deception of breathtaking audacity.
Following the days of the last World War, with a focus on Allies' strategy tensions.

In 1945 the victorious Australian men began returning from the war in Europe and the South Pacific. The women, excited and relieved to see their men return, have little knowledge of the demons that have followed them home.

programme.title: May 1940: Churchill's Finest Hour
programme.desc: For the 80th anniversary of Britain's darkest hour Dan Snow visits the House of Commons to learn about the speeches that turned the tide of WWII.

programme.title: D-Day
programme.desc: The planning for the Allied invasion on June 6th 1944 took two years and cost thousands of lives, and involved a deception of breathtaking audacity.

programme.title: Ten Days to Victory
programme.desc: The unique stories of ten very different people who were caught up in the liberation of Europe from the grip of Nazi terror are interwoven .

programme.title: Ten Days to Victory
programme.desc: The stories of 10 very different people who were caught up in the liberation of Europe from the grip of Nazi Germany during the Second World War.

programme.title: Hitler's Last Days
programme.desc: Germany is in decline. Once the world's most powerful country is now being devastated by WWII. Adolf Hitler has gone into hiding. The dreaded ruler is no longer openly supporting his people. He is slowly losing his mind.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitler's assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the 'sleeping giant', America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitler's eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With their armies fully engaged on the battlegrounds, the Allied and Axis powers focused their efforts to develop new weapons and new strategies to deliver a knockout blow to the enemy and bring about a quick end to the conflict.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: War on the two fronts caught up with Hitler leaving him on the run in mid 1943.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitler's attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific against Japan was a savage hand-to-hand, island-to-island struggle, fought by new specialist fighting units like the valiant Chindits.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Allied forces converge on Germany, uncovering the horrors of Belsen and Buchenwald, before pushing towards Berlin, where Hitler clings to power in his last desperate days.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The quest continues in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt, a deeply personal journey given Ross' grandfather fought in the North Africa campaign during World War II.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Starting in Dorset's Studland Bay, Ross searches for wrecks that tell the story of D-Day and learns that winning wasn't just a case of superior power, but also ingenuity.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Starting in Dorset's Studland Bay, Ross searches for wrecks that tell the story of D-Day and learns that winning wasn't just a case of superior power, but also ingenuity.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Situated high on a crag overlooking the river Forth, Stirling Castle has guarded the gateway to the Scottish Highlands for centuries. The residence of kings, armies and felons, its battle-scarred walls have witnessed savagery and chivalry, intrigue, alchemy, adultery and murder. Location filming, stunning aerial shots and reconstructions bring to to life its turbulent history.

programme.title: Britain's Secret Islands
programme.desc: A trip through England's south coast, diving with blue sharks, exploring the battlements of Drake's Island, learning the origins of Scouts, and checking out fossils on the Isle of Wight.

programme.title: Britain's Secret Islands
programme.desc: An exploration of Rathlin Island, including a visit to a Neolithic stone tool factory, a unique upside-down lighthouse, and a WWII shipwreck.

programme.title: Truth & Lies
programme.desc: The Politics of Religion: For thousands of years, wars and conflicts have been fought under the guise of religion. Rudolph Herzog discusses how faith has brewed conflict. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: In 2000, with well-planned ploys, seven prisoners escaped a Texas unit using strength, wit, disguises and plenty of teamwork.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Viking Camp And Iron Age Gold
programme.desc: A look at the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and groundbreaking evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isn't just a sacred site but a place where people lived and worked. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look at the biggest collection of Roman writing tablets in Britain, giving insight into what Roman London was really like. Off the coast of Kent, a dive into the English Channel reveals the biggest marine excavation since the Mary Rose, an 18th-century East India Company ship packed with silver.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Situated high on a crag overlooking the river Forth, Stirling Castle has guarded the gateway to the Scottish Highlands for centuries. The residence of kings, armies and felons, its battle-scarred walls have witnessed savagery and chivalry, intrigue, alchemy, adultery and murder. Location filming, stunning aerial shots and reconstructions bring to to life its turbulent history.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Roman Britain by studying the history of Chester, Britain's most Roman town.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In York, Professor Alice Roberts takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UK's greatest archaeological finds, and gets her hands on Viking poo.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the British Channel Islands, which were occupied by Nazi Germany for five years between 1940 and 1945.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britain's beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the world's industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Find out how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8,000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Join a team at a secret location in Dorset as they excavate an Iron Age crouched burial and in London's East End,location reporter Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt for archaeology from the more recent past as they follow the search for artefacts from World War II. They join marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise the once-in-a-lifetime find of a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. More than 2,500 Barracudas were in service during the war but not a single complete plane survives today. Naoíse Mac Sweeney joins the post excavation to reveal how the find could help bring this rare aircraft back to life.

programme.title: Truth & Lies
programme.desc: In the battle between truth and lies, money plays a decisive role. Nothing seems to be safe from the power of money and the influence of those who possess it.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Using homemade instruments to carve a hole in the roof after climbing prison pipes, eight inmates escaped from a New Mexico detention centre in 2008.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Mafia and Britain
programme.desc: Ross Kemp investigates and exposes Britain's surprising and deep-rooted links with the mafia, dating as far back as the 1920s.

programme.title: Hannibal: A March on Rome
programme.desc: Follows the story of the Carthaginian army of 90,000 soldiers as they cross the Alps to take on the might of Rome.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on his most curious walk yet, across the ancient and turbulent kingdom of Northumbria. In the seventh century, at the height of the Dark Ages, the rule of Northumbria stretched from Edinburgh to York.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath investigates and explores the strange legends, unsolved mysteries, bizarre history, unusual traditions and eccentric people of 'Weird Britain'.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

The riders who excel here are those who can stay disciplined over 113 miles.

Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

Wyatt struggles to keep his men together and safe from natural perils as well as enemy attacks.

programme.title: Teen Mom OG
programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It's Daddy Pig's birthday, but he has to go to work, so while he's away, Mummy Pig, Peppa and George prepare a special birthday surprise.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Ben & Holly's Little Kingdom

programme.desc: While Mummy and Daddy Pig attend a refresher antenatal class, Peppa and George join Miss Rabbit's Sibling School where they learn about what to expect when their new sibling arrives.

programme.desc: Peppa is so excited to meet her new baby brother or sister, but they are taking forever to arrive until suddenly Mummy Pig announces the baby is coming, now!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The nurse is popping around, and she decides that today's visit should be all about how to hold a baby. The nurse shows Peppa a special way for a small person to safely hold a baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.desc: Peppa and Suzy are excited about the sleepover and sit up in bed talking about what they will do when they grow up.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It's raining and Grandpa Rabbit built a rocket pack. As he shows it to Peppa and George, he ends up on top of a snowy mountain. Miss Hare goes to rescue him, but Grandpa Rabbit rescues them all.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.desc: Daddy Piggy arrives in a new electric car. The family goes for a ride and are surprised when the car talks and sings.

programme.desc: It's Halloween, but will a curse on the theatre stop the community putting on their show? Taylor the lifeguard arrives in Windsor Gardens, and just in time to save the Mooncake festival with Paddington.

programme.sub-title: Paddington's Birthday Gift Hunt; Paddington's Burn's Night Haggis Hunt
programme.desc: Taylor reveals its his birthday, but how can his friends make it special when they had no idea? It's Burn's Night and Mrs Bird teaches Paddington about a rare creature called a Wild Haggis, but will it visit them?

programme.sub-title: Paddington's Earth Day Mission; Paddington's Special Spring Harvest Celebration
programme.desc: For Earth Day, Paddington would really like to plant a sapling. But what is a good place for an oak tree? Baaz would like to share a special Indian harvest festival with everyone, but he has forgotten the invitations.

programme.sub-title: Paddington's Special Visitor; Paddington Celebrates Hanukkah

programme.sub-title: Paddington's Adventure Club Cracks The Case! / Paddington And Aunt Lucy's Special Recipe

programme.desc: SunnyCat invites Gabby to a mermaid sparkle party in Mermaid-Lantis, but to attend, she needs to take MerCat's blue crystal and her special sparkle.

programme.desc: A Cake sale is organised to mend the magic school but Queen Thistle is baking and the no-one can eat her cakes.

programme.desc: Lucy celebrates her birthday with an Elf and Fairy party and Ben, Holly and friends are invited.

programme.title: Ben & Holly's Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Rainbows are a pirate's best friend, if they're looking for a pot of gold. Captain Squid is hiding treasure though, so Rainbows are this pirate's worst enemy.

programme.desc: Suzy and Peppa start a Secret Club - going on secret missions, and doing secret things, but soon everyone wants to join.

programme.desc: Playgroup is having a pet competition and everyone has brought their pets. Dr Hamster is the judge, but it is so hard to choose the best pet.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa looks after Madame Gazelle's guinea pigs Ginger and Brian while she goes on holiday, but prove to be more trouble for Mummy and Daddy Pig than they think.

programme.title: Blue's Clues & You!
programme.desc: Going to New York City to see Rainbow Puppy perform; playing Blue's Clues to figure out what instrument is missing from the track before her concert begins.

programme.title: Circle Square
programme.desc: Vanessa the Dragon, owner of the rainbow lighthouse, helps out her neighbours with their everyday dilemmas. She helps different people from one of the nine households on Circle Square island.

programme.title: Circle Square
programme.desc: Caylee wants to organise a surprise birthday for yeti Grindle. With Vanessa and Ma Ruffle's help, she plans a big, bold, brash birthday celebration, just the way the likes it.

programme.title: Circle Square
programme.desc: Wizarding architect Dilys is designing a new magical tower, but also writing a scroll to granny, cooking buns, magically watering houseplants, and now she's got hiccups too.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The Detective introduces his new friend, the superhero Super Potato who teaches children the importance of eating fruits and vegetables every day, and later helps the Detective out of a complicated situation.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Love, Honor, Betray
programme.desc: Jeanne and Stephen Trantel have the dream life. But when Stephen loses his job after 9/11, their relationship splinters, and Jeanne suspects he is hiding a terrible secret - until an unexpected call reveals the shocking truth.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: 14-year-old Ashley Summers vanishes in 2007. The mystery deepens when the FBI connects her case to two other young women missing from her neighbourhood.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Diamond Bradley mystifies her family when she suddenly disappears; detectives hope surveillance footage will lead them to the missing girl, but the trail Diamond leaves behind is more terrifying than anyone expected.

programme.desc: The relatives of murderers tell their stories, discussing how the crimes affected their families and whether there were any signs of murderous intentions beforehand.

programme.desc: In Colorado, the idyllic life of spouses Harold and Toni Henthorn is shattered by a deadly hiking accident. But their happy home videos conceal a deadly predator.

programme.desc: A mother raises the alarm when her daughter goes missing from the Colorado house she had recently moved into. While Det. Ben Endreson is investigating, an unusual suspect comes forward to confess her involvement, but her story doesn't quite add up.

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Examining a devastating murder case of a girl whose younger sister was also dying of terminal cancer, and a story about an unscrupulous gang of puppy traders who were selling sick and dying puppies.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: A student suddenly vanishes after her shift at work. However, CCTV footage shows her last movements, revealing the chilling reason for her disappearance.

programme.title: The Killer In My Family
programme.desc: Relatives of serial killer Robert Maudsley reveal how his shocking crimes impacted the family. Were there any signs of his murderous intentions?

programme.title: Britain's Deadliest Kids

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: A woman's lively get-together at her local bar ends with a baffling murder.

programme.desc: Examining a devastating murder case of a girl whose younger sister was also dying of terminal cancer, and a story about an unscrupulous gang of puppy traders who were selling sick and dying puppies.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: An heiress (Diana Hyland) turns to a greedy mystic after her fiance (Jeremy Slate) dies in a mining mishap.

programme.title: Lou Reed - Transformer (Classic Album)
programme.desc: Transformer is considered to be one of the best albums of the 1970s. This documentary examines it with Lou Reed and engineer Ken Scott talking about Perfect Day and Vicious among others; Herbie Flowers shows how the base line on Walk on the Wild Side was recorded and Dave Stewart talks of Lou's influence on him.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: To be a Hollywood legend is to fly too close to the sun, to embody the dreams of millions while placing your fate in their hearts. This programme celebrates the lives of those who soared the highest.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Anna Chancellor, Stephen Graham & Beverley Knight
programme.desc: Another spot in the semi-final is up for grabs as nine more fledgling artists get set to paint stars Anna Chancellor, Stephen Graham and Beverley Knight.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Gossip columnist Lionel Brewster lets fly with his witty one-liners and his victims want revenge. Luckily two young actors, down on their luck, are on hand to offer their clients much more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: A Girl Can't Always Have Everything
programme.desc: Two struggling theatre actresses have very different off-stage personas. Suzy is a gold-digger who soon begins milking the theatre's new backer for expensive gifts, but when he then wants to marry her, she calls on her more mild-mannered friend Pat for help. How loyal is she?

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: The loyal widow (Patricia Owens) of a man killed in a mountain-climbing accident recovers his body after 20 years.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: An angry young man (Clint Kimbrough) accurately predicts an impending death. With Amy Douglass and Norma Crane.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of Gene Wilder, the charismatic star of `Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory', `Blazing Saddles' and `Young Frankenstein'.

programme.title: I Am Richard Pryor
programme.desc: Richard Pryor drew the line between comedy and tragedy as thin as possible, volatile yet vulnerable, crass but sensitive, streetwise and cocky, Pryor did not simply tell stories, he brought them to vivid life.

programme.title: Wonderland: Science Fiction in the Atomic Age
programme.desc: ...Atomic Age. Mary Shelley to Isaac Asimov: Mary Shelley's Frankenstein is often seen as the first sci-fi work, joined by early pieces from More, Swift, Wells and Huxley.

programme.title: The Lion in Winter
programme.desc: An epic historical drama set in the 12th century in which Henry II summons his fragmented family to a reunion. There follows a series of battles, both political and personal, as his wife Eleanor and her three sons scheme to further their own claim to the king's empire.

programme.title: Discovering Romance on Film
programme.desc: Film critics Ian Nathan, Bonnie Greer, Neil Norman and Stephen Armstrong come together to discuss their favourite romance movies of all time, including Casablanca and Coming to America.

programme.title: Painting Birds with Jim and Nancy Moir
programme.desc: Jim and Nancy are heading West, creating beautiful art in Ireland as they search for the Little Tern.

programme.title: Venom: The Last Dance
programme.desc: Eddie Brock and Venom must make a devastating decision as they're pursued by a mysterious military man and alien monsters from Venom's home world.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: Street Fighter
programme.desc: The cynical commander of Allied Nations forces has to prevent a warlord from taking over the world. With 63 hostages held in his seemingly impregnable fortress, the warlord is demanding an outrageously hefty ransom.

programme.title: The Legend of Tarzan
programme.desc: Lured from Victorian England to the wilds of the Congo, Tarzan must return to the jungle to save his captive wife from King Leopold's devious envoy.

programme.title: Kraven the Hunter
programme.desc: Kraven's complex relationship with his ruthless father starts him down a path of vengeance, motivating him to become not only the greatest hunter in the world, but also one of its most feared.

programme.title: Days of Thunder
programme.desc: A hotshot rookie stock car driver sets his sights on the professional NASCAR circuit and the prestigious Daytona 500.

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Spider-Man's identity is revealed to everyone, and he can no longer separate his normal life from his superhero life. When he asks Doctor Strange for help, it forces him to discover what it means to be him.

programme.title: Shazam!
programme.desc: Billy Batson is a streetwise 14-year-old who can magically transform into the adult superhero Shazam simply by shouting out one word. His newfound powers soon get put to the test when he squares off against the evil Dr. Thaddeus Sivana.

programme.title: The Running Man
programme.desc: Desperate for money to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards joins the top-rated show on television, "The Running Man," a deadly competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins.

programme.title: Kong: Skull Island
programme.desc: Scientists, soldiers and adventurers encounter the mighty Kong and other monstrous creatures after travelling to an uncharted island in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: Fuze
programme.desc: London undergoes a citywide evacuation when a World War II bomb is unearthed at a construction site. Amid the escalating chaos, a daring criminal operation is set in motion -- one that uses the evacuation as a cover for a meticulously planned heist.

programme.title: Jurassic World Rebirth
programme.desc: Skilled operatives travel to an island research facility to secure life-saving DNA from dinosaurs. As the top-secret mission becomes more and more dangerous, they soon make a sinister, shocking discovery that's been hidden from the world for decades.

programme.title: William Tell
programme.desc: In 14th Century Switzerland, a once peaceful hunter leads his people in rebellion after his family and country are threatened by a tyrannical Austrian King. As the Holy Roman Empire crumbles, the quiet lives of the Swiss peasants are upended.

programme.title: William Tell
programme.desc: In 14th Century Switzerland, a once peaceful hunter leads his people in rebellion after his family and country are threatened by a tyrannical Austrian King. As the Holy Roman Empire crumbles, the quiet lives of the Swiss peasants are upended.

programme.title: Police Academy
programme.desc: Misfits realise their dreams of becoming cops when a mayoral decree forces the police academy to open its doors to all.

programme.title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch
programme.desc: Six years after defeating the Gremlins, Billy and Kate have grown up and moved to New York. However, Billy stumbles across Gizmo in a genetics lab and when he gets wet, all hell breaks loose, and the stage is set for more deliciously bad taste humour.

programme.title: The Hangover Part III
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Doug take Alan on a road trip to help him through a personal crisis.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: A fast-talking cop trails his best friend's killer from the alleys of Detroit to the posh galleries of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: Bridget Jones: Mad About the Boy
programme.desc: After jumping back into the dating pool, single mother Bridget Jones finds herself caught between a younger man and her son's science teacher.

programme.title: American Pie
programme.desc: Believing it essential before they go to college, four teenage boys vow to lose their virginity and enter into a pact, each attempting to be the first to do the deed as their efforts are met with varying levels of success and failure.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop II
programme.desc: The hard-nosed Detroit cop from the original film returns to Los Angeles to help solve another case. This time he must direct his efforts towards unraveling the Alphabet Crimes, a series of robberies committed by leather-jacketed punks. The investigation puts him on the trail of an illegal arms dealer and his hit lady.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality
programme.desc: An uncouth FBI agent goes undercover in the Miss United States beauty pageant as a contestant to flush out a terrorist who plans to bomb the event. The hard-nosed cop, who prides herself on her toughness, must discard her trainers and feminise her behaviour if she is to avoid blowing her cover.

programme.title: American Pie 2
programme.desc: Action-packed sequel in which a group of friends are reunited after their freshman year of college and embark upon a series of comical adventures when they rent a summer house on Lake Michigan.

programme.title: Liar Liar
programme.desc: A slick attorney fails to show up for his son's fifth birthday, prompting the boy to make a wish that his father would tell the truth for just one day. Miraculously, the wish is granted and for the next 24 hours, the formerly mendacious lawyer is unable to tell a single lie.

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle
programme.desc: Guided by a rebellious and charismatic coach, British ski jumper Michael "Eddie" Edwards trains for the 1988 Winter Olympics in Calgary, Alberta.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: A rich property developer wants to take over an exclusive country club and so challenges its pompous president to a game of golf leading to utter mayhem, and a stream of surreal and crude visual gags.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders' side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
programme.desc: Mahoney and friends have graduated from the Police Academy and are issued with their first assignments. As before, their cheerfulness and devotion to duty outweighs any shortcomings, but are they ready to do battle with a band of graffiti daubing terrorists?

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
programme.desc: Mahoney and friends have graduated from the Police Academy and are issued with their first assignments. As before, their cheerfulness and devotion to duty outweighs any shortcomings, but are they ready to do battle with a band of graffiti daubing terrorists?

programme.title: The End of the Affair
programme.desc: On a rainy London night in 1946, novelist Maurice Bendrix has a chance meeting with Henry Miles, the husband of his ex-mistress Sarah, who abruptly ended their affair two years before. Bendrix's obsession with Sarah is rekindled; he succumbs to his own jealousy and arranges to have her followed. As the investigation progresses, we learn the reason for their separation.

programme.title: Burning an Illusion
programme.desc: A young British-born black woman begins to question her attitude to love and life, and her desire for middle-class respectability and security through marriage.

programme.title: Cicada
programme.desc: Ben, a young bisexual man, comes out to the world and develops an intense relationship with Sam, a closeted man struggling with deep wounds of his own. As summer progresses and their intimacy grows, secrets from the past come to light.

programme.title: ...And Justice for All
programme.desc: Satirical drama in which an idealistic lawyer who tries to win cases for clients trapped in a hypocritical and corrupt legal system finds himself defending a venal and self-righteous judge accused of rape. The judge wrongly assumes the lawyer's ideals and disdain for the courts will drive him to get an acquittal, despite the evidence and the loathing they have for each other.

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby and his dying mother. Her dreary life is interrupted when her schoolgirl crush arrives back in town.

programme.title: Birdy
programme.desc: A soldier returns from Vietnam to find his boyhood friend, also a fellow soldier, in a military hospital, having suffered a mental breakdown in which he has become detached from reality and imagines that he is a bird.

programme.title: Burning an Illusion
programme.desc: A young British-born black woman begins to question her attitude to love and life, and her desire for middle-class respectability and security through marriage.

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal and the family face financial trouble.

programme.title: The Friend
programme.desc: Iris finds her comfortable New York City life thrown into disarray after her closest friend and mentor bequeaths her a Great Dane named Apollo. Yet as Iris finds herself unexpectedly bonding with Apollo, she begins to come to terms with her past.

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption
programme.desc: Andy Dufresne and Ellis Boyd `Red' Redding are two prisoners serving long sentences in the vicious Shawshank jail. The two come to terms with their imprisonment while surviving the sadistic acts of prison warden Samuel Norton, and for both, a different sort of redemption awaits.

programme.title: The Brutalist
programme.desc: Escaping postwar Europe, a visionary architect comes to America to rebuild his life, his career, and his marriage. On his own in a strange new country, he settles in Pennsylvania, where a wealthy and prominent industrialist recognises his talent.

programme.title: The End of the Affair
programme.desc: On a rainy London night in 1946, novelist Maurice Bendrix has a chance meeting with Henry Miles, the husband of his ex-mistress Sarah, who abruptly ended their affair two years before. Bendrix's obsession with Sarah is rekindled; he succumbs to his own jealousy and arranges to have her followed. As the investigation progresses, we learn the reason for their separation.

programme.title: ...And Justice for All
programme.desc: Satirical drama in which an idealistic lawyer who tries to win cases for clients trapped in a hypocritical and corrupt legal system finds himself defending a venal and self-righteous judge accused of rape. The judge wrongly assumes the lawyer's ideals and disdain for the courts will drive him to get an acquittal, despite the evidence and the loathing they have for each other.

programme.title: ...And Justice for All
programme.desc: Satirical drama in which an idealistic lawyer who tries to win cases for clients trapped in a hypocritical and corrupt legal system finds himself defending a venal and self-righteous judge accused of rape. The judge wrongly assumes the lawyer's ideals and disdain for the courts will drive him to get an acquittal, despite the evidence and the loathing they have for each other.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Mouse Hunt
programme.desc: Slapstick tale which follows the misfortunes of two hard-up brothers whose plans to renovate and sell a valuable building are jeopardised by a rogue mouse. The brothers soon find themselves pulling out all the stops in order to evict their tiny resident.

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: The Wild Robot
programme.desc: Shipwrecked on a deserted island, a robot named Roz must learn to adapt to its new surroundings. Building relationships with the native animals, Roz soon develops a parental bond with an orphaned gosling.

programme.title: Pokémon Detective Pikachu
programme.desc: A young man joins forces with Detective Pikachu to unravel the mystery behind his father's disappearance. Chasing clues through the streets of Ryme City, the dynamic duo soon discover a devious plot that poses a threat to the Pokémon universe.

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle
programme.desc: Four teens embark on the adventure of a lifetime when a video game magically transports them to the jungle and transforms them into adults.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3
programme.desc: Sonic, Knuckles and Tails reunite to battle Shadow, a mysterious new enemy with powers unlike anything they've faced before. With their abilities outmatched in every way, they seek out an unlikely alliance to stop Shadow and protect the planet.

programme.title: Sing
programme.desc: A pig, a mouse, a porcupine, a gorilla, an elephant and other animals gather at a koala's theatre for a singing competition.

programme.title: Mr. Peabody & Sherman
programme.desc: Mr. Peabody, the most accomplished canine in the world, and his boy, Sherman, use a time machine called the Wabac to embark on outrageous adventures. However, when Sherman takes the Wabac without permission to impress his friend Penny, he accidentally rips a hole in the universe and causes havoc with world history. It's up to Mr. Peabody to mount a rescue and prevent the past, present and future from being permanently altered. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: Mouse Hunt
programme.desc: Slapstick tale which follows the misfortunes of two hard-up brothers whose plans to renovate and sell a valuable building are jeopardised by a rogue mouse. The brothers soon find themselves pulling out all the stops in order to evict their tiny resident.

programme.title: Daddy Day Care
programme.desc: Two fathers get laid off from their well-paid jobs and are forced to stay at home to look after their kids. Seeing the opportunity to make money, the two of them open their own day-care centre. With very little experience and some fairly unconventional ideas on child care, can they really hope to compete against the prim and proper Chapman Academy, which has monopolised the child care industry in their town?

programme.title: Free Willy
programme.desc: A heart-warming tale about a troubled young boy and his friendship with a captive orca whale named Willy. On bad terms with his new foster parents and set to work in an aquarium under court order, the boy turns to the whale for companionship. When he uncovers a cruel plot by its owner to kill the whale in order to claim a big insurance payout, the lad becomes determined to save Willy's life and release him back into the wild.

programme.title: Free Willy
programme.desc: A heart-warming tale about a troubled young boy and his friendship with a captive orca whale named Willy. On bad terms with his new foster parents and set to work in an aquarium under court order, the boy turns to the whale for companionship. When he uncovers a cruel plot by its owner to kill the whale in order to claim a big insurance payout, the lad becomes determined to save Willy's life and release him back into the wild.

programme.title: Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: Martial arts romp about a TV chef who becomes embroiled in a drug lord's search for an incriminating video. The kung-fu fighting cook steps in to save an investigative journalist on the run from a gang of murderous drug dealers, resulting in a potentially fatal mix-up involving her incriminating tape of the gangsters and a children's video.

programme.title: Rumble in the Bronx
programme.desc: A Hong Kong cop on holiday in New York puts his martial arts skills to good use when he helps out a friend whose shop is targeted by a vicious street gang. Martial arts hero Jackie Chan, as always, does all his own stunts.

programme.title: The Warriors
programme.desc: Wrongly accused of assassinating top gang leader Cyrus at a rally, Coney Islanders The Warriors have to fight their way home over the course of one long, violent night.

programme.title: Shenandoah
programme.desc: A man's neutrality during the American Civil War is tested by his daughter's marriage and his son's capture by Union forces.

programme.title: Demolition Man
programme.desc: Violence has been outlawed in the politically correct regime of 2032, and the only weapons in existence are consigned to a museum, but when an escaped psychopath heads straight for the museum, the authorities decide the only effective recourse they have is to thaw out a violent, cryogenically frozen cop.

programme.title: First Knight
programme.desc: Dissatisfied with King Arthur's rule of peace and freedom, Malagant, an erstwhile ally, turns against the Round Table and establishes a hostile domain. However, he goes too far when he kidnaps the queen and imprisons her in an appalling dungeon. The special skills of the glittering and impetuous swordsman Lancelot must be called upon to effect her rescue.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: Martial arts romp about a TV chef who becomes embroiled in a drug lord's search for an incriminating video. The kung-fu fighting cook steps in to save an investigative journalist on the run from a gang of murderous drug dealers, resulting in a potentially fatal mix-up involving her incriminating tape of the gangsters and a children's video.

programme.title: Patriot Games
programme.desc: While visiting London with his family, Jack Ryan foils an IRA attempt to assassinate a government minister, killing one of the attackers in the process. Swearing vengeance, the IRA's Sean Miller follows Ryan and his family to their seaside home in America where he and his followers plan to exact their revenge.

programme.title: Batman
programme.desc: In an attempt to avenge the violent death of his parents, Bruce Wayne fights to rid the city of crime and in particular, his nemesis the Joker. However, his defences come down when an attractive journalist tries to track down the truth behind sightings of a huge bat-like figure in the sky.

programme.title: The Mummy
programme.desc: The curse of an ancient mummy protects buried treasure in the Egyptian desert. An American mercenary who knows of the secret location is rescued from a Cairo prison by an English historian and her brother, and together they set off on a hunt. Along the way, the trio encounter flesh-eating scarab beetles, killer sandstorms, and reincarnated demons.

programme.title: The Firm
programme.desc: A young lawyer joins a small but prestigious law firm only to find out that most of their clients are on the wrong side of the law. The company is helping to launder mob money, get clients off charges and even murder partners who threaten to blow their cover, but when the FBI come calling to gather evidence on the lawyer's colleagues, he is caught between a rock and a hard place, juggling his life and his liberty.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Fear Seekers
programme.desc: This Cinema List is for fear seekers. From The Shining and Weapons to Jaws, brace yourself for relentless tension and terrifying moments that keep you on the edge of your seat.

programme.title: The Towering Inferno
programme.desc: At the opening party of a colossal, but poorly constructed office building, a massive fire breaks out that threatens to destroy the tower and everyone in it.

programme.title: The Towering Inferno
programme.desc: At the opening party of a colossal, but poorly constructed office building, a massive fire breaks out that threatens to destroy the tower and everyone in it.

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Quarterback Cameron Cade trains at an isolated compound with his hero, Isaiah White. As training progresses, Isaiah's charisma turns into something darker, sending Cam down a disorienting rabbit hole that may cost him more than he ever bargained for.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
Tafiti: Across the Desert
programme.desc: When his grandfather gets bitten by a snake, a young meerkat embarks on an adventure across the desert to find the magical flower that can cure him.

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie
programme.desc: When Gabby's dollhouse, her most prized possession, ends up in the hands of an eccentric cat lady named Vera, she sets off on an adventure through the real world to get the Gabby Cats back together before it's too late.

programme.title: The Internship
programme.desc: A highly trained assassin is ready to dismantle the top-secret CIA programme that stole her youth. Assembling fellow operatives, she leads a bloody uprising that forces the CIA to unleash equally lethal forces to stop them.

programme.title: The Bride!
programme.desc: In the 1930s Chicago, groundbreaking scientist Dr. Euphronious brings a murdered young woman back to life to be a companion for Frankenstein's monster. What happens next is beyond what either of them could ever have imagined.

programme.title: Dead Man's Wire
programme.desc: On Feb. 8, 1977, disgruntled real-estate developer Tony Kiritsis takes mortgage broker Richard Hall hostage, keeping him tied up in his apartment with a sawn-off shotgun pointed at his chest.

programme.title: The Plague
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp, his anxiety spiralling into psychological turmoil over the summer.

programme.title: Fuze
programme.desc: London undergoes a citywide evacuation when a World War II bomb is unearthed at a construction site. Amid the escalating chaos, a daring criminal operation is set in motion -- one that uses the evacuation as a cover for a meticulously planned heist.

programme.title: Sisu: Road to Revenge
programme.desc: Korpi dismantles the house where his family was murdered and loads it on a truck to rebuild it somewhere safe. He soon finds himself in a violent cross-country chase as the Red Army commander who killed his family comes back to finish the job.

programme.title: Dead Man's Wire
programme.desc: On Feb. 8, 1977, disgruntled real-estate developer Tony Kiritsis takes mortgage broker Richard Hall hostage, keeping him tied up in his apartment with a sawn-off shotgun pointed at his chest.

programme.title: World War II With Tom Hanks
programme.desc: As Hitler's reach extends beyond Germany and darkness descends on Europe, his hateful rhetoric leads to anti-Jewish persecution and ultimately, genocide.

programme.title: Digging for Britain

Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeare's rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.desc: In the Luangwa Valley lies a grove of Ebony trees, home to a troop of baboons. But, there are dangers here as the resident leopard is powerful and agile enough to climb trees.

programme.title: Into the Wild Colombia
programme.desc: Growing up in the rainforests in the heart of Colombia, a young capuchin monkey has to master his climbing skills, learn how to find food and to distinguish friend from foe.

programme.title: Secrets of Wild Australia
programme.desc: The snakes of Australia are as diverse and striking as the landscape they inhabit. There are more than 300 different species here, some harmless to humans, others deadly.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The next stage of lemur introductions keeps the primate care staff on their toes, while gibbons Kim and Tien head back to their familiar stomping ground.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: In Dao Tien a group of misbehaving doucs need to be relocated and female golden-cheeked gibbon Tungs release is hampered by the weather.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: The sun rules everything on this arid peninsula, especially in July. But those who call this desert home, such as camels, lizards and rock hyraxes, have adapted to beat the heat.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains
Secrets of Wild Australia
programme.desc: The snakes of Australia are as diverse and striking as the landscape they inhabit. There are more than 300 different species here, some harmless to humans, others deadly.

programme.title: Into the Wild Colombia
programme.desc: Growing up in the rainforests in the heart of Colombia, a young capuchin monkey has to master his climbing skills, learn how to find food and to distinguish friend from foe.

programme.title: Brazil Untamed
programme.desc: A look at the wildlife of Brazil.

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: A subtropical realm of staggering diversity exists on the eastern coast of Africa. A mangrove forest experiences the high and low tides.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: The sun rules everything on this arid peninsula, especially in July. But those who call this desert home, such as camels, lizards and rock hyraxes, have adapted to beat the heat.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains
Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Introducing some of the big characters including `class clown' Valentino and `Big Boy' Beni. There is also an emergency arrival with teenage mum Clara and her baby Clarita and vets round up the older orangutans, who are now ready to be released.

programme.title: Valley of Flood and Fire
programme.desc: It's the end of November in the Luangwa Valley, and the rains have just begun. It marks the end of one of the toughest dry season on record, and triggers a transition from parched wilderness to luscious wetland.

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.sub-title: The Okavango: Lifeblood of the Kalahari
programme.desc: Take a look at one of Africa's most abundant and diverse wildlife areas, the Okavango Delta. Investigate the way the delta functions and learn about the variety of interesting species that live in the area.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Concerns for chimp Jestah who has a lump on his jaw. Lemurs Fennel and Ben trial life with the guenons but youngster Biff proves a bit full on.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The large playroom at the orangutan nursery is a hit with youngsters Kiwi and Sibu, while chimp Clin gets a manicure for safety rather than appearance.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: `Class Clown' Valentino is busy showing off, and the staff decide that `Big Boy' Beni needs to lose weight. Teenage mum Clara and her baby Clarita face a return to island life and they welcome a new pupil: Alba, the only reported albino orangutan.

programme.title: Valley of Flood and Fire
programme.desc: It's the end of November in the Luangwa Valley, and the rains have just begun. It marks the end of one of the toughest dry season on record, and triggers a transition from parched wilderness to luscious wetland.

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: The plains and woodlands of the Luangwa Valley are the wildest and most merciless of all neighbourhoods where duels between predator and prey are played out both day and night. For the animals that call this home, it's a daily battle to stay alive.

programme.title: Into the Wild Colombia
programme.desc: From Colombia's high plains of the Páramo to its tropical forests, hummingbirds have evolved into an extraordinary number of species. Whether battling freezing rain or jungle predators, each mother must struggle to build a nest, protect her eggs.

programme.title: Secrets of Wild Australia
programme.desc: This wild dog has been on Australia's shores for at least three and a half thousand years and is believed to be descended from the Asian wolf.

programme.title: Most Haunted

The team spend the first night on the good side, but is there such a thing as a `good' ghost?

programme.title: UK Border Force

Plus, in Yeovil, raids on a bingo hall uncover illegal workers, while five Iraqis are discovered in the back of a lorry in Calais.

programme.title: Motorway Patrol

Plus, an armed response unit is called to a dangerous domestic incident with more than one knife involved.

programme.title: Chicago Fire
programme.desc: A new chief shakes things up at Firehouse 51; Violet and Novak work to uncover the truth behind a car crash; Cruz keeps a watchful eye on Severide and Damon's growing bond.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: After a string of armed robberies target various businesses, the team investigates a suspect who leads them to something much bigger; OA worries about his future when his investments begin to go south.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: When an off-duty diplomatic security agent is fatally shot in New York City trying to apprehend someone, the team investigates if there's a connection to his time working in Croatia.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A candidate for the next mayor of New York could see her election hopes dashed when Goren and Eames investigate her husband on suspicion of murder.

programme.title: Police 24/7
programme.desc: Police must figure out the rightful owner of a stolen bike; a pizza delivery man suffers serious injuries while on the job; a drunk man goes home to the wrong house.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A doctor is killed at his son's bar mitzvah, and the first suspect is his estranged wife.

programme.title: The Rookie
programme.desc: Officer Nolan discovers his confidential informant has gone back to her criminal ways. Lucy's friendship with Jackson is tested.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: The abduction of a young woman leads the team back to a cold case from 18 years ago, which pushes Isobel to reconnect with her old partner Jake and take another shot at solving one of her first cases at the bureau.

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: The team jumps into action when analyst Kelly Moran is taken hostage.

programme.title: The Rookie

programme.desc: Eleanor and Rob from Hereford would love an eco-friendly garden for themselves and their two daughters to enjoy. Of primary importance to the couple is that their garden is low/no carbon and that it is friendly to the environment. They would like the garden build to be based on a `circular economy' ie. any carbon spent would be offset by reusing materials. They want lots of habitats for birds, butterflies and bees and to use as many natural materials as possible.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Rick Stein joins in the ancient festival of Wassailing, tries out the famous ice rink at Eden, enjoys the company of Port Isaac's Fisherman's Friends and cooks his own goose. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The lads rev it up when they race a BMW 6 Series, Porsche 911 and Jaguar XKR along Pendine Sands in Wales. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy reviews the Nissan Cube and Renault Clio 182, James tries out a modern take on a timeless classic - the Jaguar XJS, while Richard Hammond road tests the Cadillac CTS and investigates alternative ways of fuelling your car. Terry Wogan is this week's Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob decide to try and catch a legendary Pike, which is perhaps not the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cosy cottage where they are staying, they invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. Facing the future, they write a eulogy for each other as the sun sets on their final fishing expedition.

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Starting at the spectacular Giant's Causeway in Northern Ireland, Simon travels down the east coast to the great and rapidly changing cities of Belfast and Dublin. He ends his journey in the stunning Wicklow Mountains.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si look across the Channel to see how our French cousins have influenced the way we cook and eat over the centuries. The Bikers prepare a delicious roast chicken with forty cloves of garlic and a delicate mille feuille, they also show us that Britain is now leading the way in many traditional `French' areas for gastronomy, from award winning English wines to outstanding Scottish garlic.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick Stein meets young chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant in Cornwall. Here they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small heard of Mangalitsa pigs.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy tests the new Ford GT and the Toyota Prius - an electric/petrol engine hybrid; Richard celebrates the Rover V8 engine, and James reviews the Maserati Quattroporte. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Martin Clunes.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to the picturesque River Usk in Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. Their quarry is the wild brown trout. They discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon continues his journey in Libya, a country well off the tourist trail and torn apart by revolution, insurgents from the so-called Islamic State and western air strikes. He then heads to Tunisia, a country that unlike its neighbour, has long been a tourist destination. Heading deep into the desert, the backdrop for scenes in the `Star Wars' films, Simon visits the fortress village of Chenini.

programme.desc: Janine and Ryan's wedded bliss is short-lived when a shock revelation threatens to tear them apart. Alfie opens the Vic for business, but not as the Square's residents know it.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Janine makes a call with devastating consequences for Stacey. Alfie's love nest fails to win Kat around, and Lauren discovers distance doesn't always make the heart grow fonder.

programme.title: Tenko
programme.desc: Ex-Doctor Who companion Louise Jameson has a tough old time of it in the gripping drama set in a World War II POW camp, and new Dutch women prisoners arrive at the camp laden with possessions.

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Frank is playing 18 holes on Ballarat golf course when another club member, a local newspaper reporter, turns up dead in one of the bunkers. Frank and Blake soon discover there is a lot more to local politics than meets the eye.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lovejoy overhears a row at a society wedding between the groom and the groom's father-in-law, a rich landowner. That night Lovejoy discovers the newly-married man has been murdered, but nobody believes him.

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom turns his attentions to Diana and suggests that they shack up together, but when Diana spurns his offer, he turns his attentions to a new resident.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Auntie Wainwright has a new line in all things equestrian, causing quite a stir. However, Compo, Clegg and Truly have other things on their minds.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.desc: Compo discovers Nora Batty's secret scar, and the chaps meet Billy Hardcastle dressed as Robin Hood.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Who's Thrown Her Tom Cruise Photographs Away?
programme.desc: When Marina is stood up by Howard, she decides to give up on men. Truly sees a way of making Marina jealous.

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: With Maria and the Fallen Madonna caught by Von Klinkerhoffen, a brave rescue attempt is planned. In fact, three brave rescue attempts.

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene has successfully got the painting back so Michelle concentrates on her plan to get the airmen away in the ancient aeroplane.

programme.title: New Tricks

programme.desc: Howard secretly buys an old van and asks Wesley to customise it for him to make it comfortable for long journeys. Unfortunately, Compo insists on testing the vehicle on its maiden voyage.

programme.sub-title: From Audrey Nash to the Window Dilhooley

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: The most extreme and wild parts of New Zealand are in the South Island, which lie towards Antarctica, in the path of the tempestuous `roaring forties'. This is home to some of the most rapidly rising mountains in the world, the Southern Alps. From hyper-intelligent parrots, to sinister snails with teeth and magical constellations of glow-worms, this is the story of New Zealand's wildest places and its most resilient pioneers, all of who must embrace radical solutions to survive.

programme.desc: A look at bizarre hybrids within the animal kingdom, including the pizzly bear which is a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which has come into existence because of global warming.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James reveals the cars that turned post-war Germany and Japan into motoring powerhouses at the expense of Britain and the US. On his travels he encounters classic E-Types, Mustangs and the German and Japanese upstarts. He also has an unfortunate encounter with an Austin Allegro - the car that helped destroy the British car industry.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James goes off-road to tell the story of how the 4x4 conquered the world. From the wartime jeep and the global struggle between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, to the white-knuckle world of rallying and boy racers. James conquers Mount Fuji and races through the sun scorched Mojave Desert to decide which is the ultimate people's 4x4.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James explores the wilder shores of motoring to discover what happened to the cars of the future that we were promised. From improbable steam cars and ludicrous jet turbines he reveals how the petrol engine and the power it gave us came to dominate the 20th century. He'll take to the waves in an amphibious car, risk his life at the wheel of a notorious electric scooter and take a hair raising trip in the fastest driverless car on earth.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: A perfect diecast replica of a Merchant Navy steam locomotive is the task for Hornby's design team. Engineer Chris tries to capture the authentic sounds of a Light Pacific loco.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: Experienced designer Carl has a challenge on his hands attempting to recreate a WWI biplane as a 1/48th scale Corgi model.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Isolated since the time of the dinosaurs, New Zealand's wildlife has been left to its own devices - with surprising consequences. Its ancient forests are still stalked by predators from the Jurassic era. It's also one of the most geologically active countries on Earth. From Kiwis with their giant eggs, to forest-dwelling penguins and helicopter-riding sheep dogs, meet the astonishing creatures and resilient people who must rise to the challenges of their beautiful, dramatic and demanding home.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: The most extreme and wild parts of New Zealand are in the South Island, which lie towards Antarctica, in the path of the tempestuous `roaring forties'. This is home to some of the most rapidly rising mountains in the world, the Southern Alps. From hyper-intelligent parrots, to sinister snails with teeth and magical constellations of glow-worms, this is the story of New Zealand's wildest places and its most resilient pioneers, all of who must embrace radical solutions to survive.

programme.desc: James explores the wilder shores of motoring to discover what happened to the cars of the future that we were promised. From improbable steam cars and ludicrous jet turbines he reveals how the petrol engine and the power it gave us came to dominate the 20th century. He'll take to the waves in an amphibious car, risk his life at the wheel of a notorious electric scooter and take a hair raising trip in the fastest driverless car on earth.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: A perfect diecast replica of a Merchant Navy steam locomotive is the task for Hornby's design team. Engineer Chris tries to capture the authentic sounds of a Light Pacific loco.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: Experienced designer Carl has a challenge on his hands attempting to recreate a WWI biplane as a 1/48th scale Corgi model.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James reveals the cars that turned post-war Germany and Japan into motoring powerhouses at the expense of Britain and the US. On his travels he encounters classic E-Types, Mustangs and the German and Japanese upstarts. He also has an unfortunate encounter with an Austin Allegro - the car that helped destroy the British car industry.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY team head to Dumfries in Scotland to meet Sue, whose partner Barry died of a sudden heart attack in 2003, leaving her suffering from depression, dealing with two young children and living in a house that was falling apart. The team enlist the aid of their very own tartan army to tackle this build and try to help get this family back on track.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team join forces with an army of local tradespeople to come to the aid of a family in Dudley. All three children in the Whitehouse family have an inherited condition called Fragile X Syndrome. The symptoms vary, but their youngest son has been hit hardest and has learning difficulties. Dad Wayne and mum Joanne attempted to build an extension to their house to try and cope.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team travel to Duxford near Cambridge. They enlist the aid of local trades to help Paul and Sarah Behan, who had just started an extension to their tiny bungalow when their young son Joshua was diagnosed with a rare and aggressive form of liver cancer. Joshua's cancer meant the extension had to be abandoned just when it became more necessary than ever.

programme.desc: After 20 years serving his country, Royal Engineer and Paratrooper Mo Morris was given a medical discharge. He has been left struggling to walk in a home which is totally unsuitable to his needs. Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team come to his rescue.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team head to Halifax to help a family whose eight-year-old son Josh is paralysed and can only access two rooms in the house. The family home needs a major overhaul and the DIY SOS Team have just nine days to pull it off.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Hashtag Homicide
programme.desc: Delving into real cases in the digital age, tracing social media clues throughout investigations to reveal how online footprints expose murder and reshape the pursuit of truth.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie and Patsy taste the fruits of freedom while Saffy is in Iraq on a humanitarian mission. Eddie's house, career and personal hygiene have gone to pot and her client list has dwindled to Emma Bunton and Queen Noor.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie is having nightmares about becoming a grandmother, as Saffy tells the father of her child the big news. Plus, Patsy and Eddie try to focus on their celebrity book club - with little success.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie is thrilled by the newest addition to her house: a panic room, equipped with champagne, toilet paper and a high-tech surveillance system. Patsy is doing her best to impress new client Minnie Driver.

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Guy attempts to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle: an incredible 110mph. He recruits a truck racer, an Olympic gold medallist, a bicycle builder and a design engineer.

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Guy attempts to set the world record for riding a motorcycle on the surface of water. His stunt hinges on Sir Isaac Newton's Third Law of Motion: that every action has an equal and opposite reaction.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree, with their own names: Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Stricken by the humiliation of defeat and the harsh terms of the Treaty of Versailles, Germany looks to one man for a resurgence of hope and dignity - Adolf Hitler.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: One of the largest Nazi bunkers ever constructed, outside the French town of Watten, a missile facility with storage for 108 missiles and two launch pads.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set up for a busy day at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, London. Objects intriguing the experts include the effects of Queen Mary's personal bodyguard and a Victorian toilet described as the owner's 'pride and joy'. The arrival of a painting of a female spy from World War One triggers the discovery of an important painting.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A second visit to the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich as the team of experts is kept busy by a huge crowd of visitors eager to learn more about their treasures. Objects under scrutiny include one of the rarest groups of medals to be seen at a Roadshow, a Lalique figure found under a hotel bed by a chambermaid, plus Fiona Bruce discovers it's true that when waiting for a bus, three can turn up at once.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport. This classic car was styled by the well known Milanese coach builder, Zagato. Zagato are famous for collaborating with high end car brands.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a classic Morgan sports car. A World War Two ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale and a Ford Model T needs a new home.

programme.desc: While the full force of the Nazi war machine sweeps across Europe, an uneasy calm settles on Britain as Churchill replaces Chamberlain as prime minister.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.sub-title: Dag Bromberg: A City in the Forest
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a complex containing more than 1,000 structures across 25sqkm.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts visit Beverley Minster in Yorkshire. Pieces under scrutiny include a valuable medieval ring dug up on a farm; two Victorian paintings given in exchange for a gambling debt; and a car rescued from a pig farm's outbuilding, which turns out to be a former rally winner once driven by Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are back for a second visit to Beverley Minster in the East Riding of Yorkshire. Hundreds of visitors pack the nave eager to see the experts. Amongst the objects caught on camera are a pair of valuable medical leech jars once used to bleed patients and a curious sideboard that hides secret drawers that took the owners 30 years to find.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso.
